So Amazon workers got their $25 wage and suddenly every other corporate overlord is scrambling to throw money at their serfs. Classic domino effect, like watching capitalism eat its own tail in real time.

The Fed sees wage increases and immediately starts hyperventilating about inflation like they've never heard of supply and demand. Because God forbid workers actually afford rent AND food in the same month.

Nothing says 'we value our human workforce' like automation stocks quadrupling the second people demand living wages. These companies went from 'essential workers' to 'essentially replaceable' faster than you can say 'self-checkout.'

Small businesses discover subscription models because apparently selling a product once isn't profitable anymore when you have to pay humans human wages. Welcome to the everything-as-a-service hellscape where you lease your burrito.
The EU threatens tariffs because American workers getting paid disrupts their precious global exploitation chain. Heaven forbid we can't manufacture poverty across international borders anymore.

Coffee shops cut hours because paying baristas decent money is apparently more expensive than losing half their business hours. Math is hard when you're used to wage slavery subsidizing your profit margins.

Some mouth-breathing customer loses their absolute mind over three extra dollars for a burrito and runs to Yelp like it's the International Court of Justice. THREE DOLLARS! That's less than the gas you burned driving to complain about it online, you absolute walnut!
